1.title: The "strangers scattered"
1.body: Peter writes to Christians spread across five regions of Asia Minor (modern Turkey), treating them as pilgrims far from their heavenly home.

1.question: According to verse 3, unto what has God begotten us again?

1.template: That the trial of your faith, being much more precious than of {0} that perisheth, though it be tried with fire

1.title: The prophets searched
1.body: Peter teaches that the ancient prophets, by the Spirit of Christ within them, testified beforehand of both the sufferings of Christ and the glory that should follow.

1.question: What commandment does Peter quote from scripture in verse 16?
options: Be ye holy; for I am holy

1.question: With what were we NOT redeemed, according to verse 18?
options: With corruptible things, as silver and gold

1.template: But with the precious blood of Christ, as of a {0} without blemish and without spot

2.title: The sincere milk of the word
2.body: Peter compares new converts to newborn babes who long for milk: so should we desire the word of God in order to grow spiritually.

2.template: To whom coming, as unto a living {0}, disallowed indeed of men, but chosen of God, and precious,

2.question: According to the scripture quoted, what does God lay in Sion?
To what does Peter tell believers to submit for the Lord's sake?
options: Only the elders of the church

2.question: Which of these commands is NOT in verse 17?

2.title: Follow his steps
2.body: Peter reminds servants who suffer unjustly that Christ also suffered for us, leaving an example that we should follow his steps.

2.template: Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the {0}, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness:

3.template: even the ornament of a meek and {0} spirit, which is in the sight of God of great price

3.prompt: In verse 6, who is cited as an example for obeying her husband, calling him lord?
Match each exhortation in verses 8–9 to its completion.

3.template: And who is he that will harm you, if ye be followers of that which is {0}?

3.prompt: In verse 15 (17 by count), the saints should be ready always to give an answer about what?
options: A reason of the hope that is in them

3.title: Preached unto the spirits in prison
3.body: Verses 18–20 teach that between His crucifixion and resurrection, Christ ministered to the spirits of the dead. For Latter-day Saints, this passage grounds the understanding of the redemption of the dead and salvation for the dead.

3.prompt: In verse 20, how many souls were saved by water in the days of Noah?

4.question: He who has ceased from sin should live the rest of his time in the flesh to what?

4.title: The gospel preached to the dead
4.body: This verse supports the restored doctrine that the gospel is preached to the dead in the spirit world, so that they may be judged according to men in the flesh and live according to God in the spirit.

4.question: With the end of all things at hand, what does Peter urge the Saints to do?
options: Be sober and watch unto prayer

4.template: for charity shall cover the multitude of {0}
Use hospitality one to another without {0}.

4.question: How does Peter say each man should minister the gift he has received?
options: As good stewards of the manifold grace of God
In exchange for the honor of men

4.title: Good stewards of God's grace
4.body: Peter teaches that every gift received should serve others: he who speaks, let him speak the oracles of God, and he who ministers, let him do it by the ability God gives, that in all things God may be glorified through Jesus Christ.

4.instructions: Match the attitude in persecution to its result.

pairs.left: Being reproached for the name of Christ
pairs.right: Happiness and the spirit of glory
Gladness when his glory is revealed

4.question: According to Peter, where is the time come that judgment must begin?
options: With the kings of the earth

5.title: The chief Shepherd
5.text: Peter calls Christ the "chief Shepherd," echoing the image of Jesus as the Good Shepherd who cares for the flock. Elders shepherd, but Christ is the supreme Shepherd to whom they answer.

5.question: What will the elders receive when the chief Shepherd shall appear?
options: A crown of glory that fadeth not away

5.template: Casting all your {0} upon him; for he careth for you.

5.question: How does Peter describe the devil, the adversary of the saints?
options: As a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour
As a wolf among the sheep
As a serpent in the wilderness
As a thief in the night

5.title: Babylon
5.text: Many scholars understand "Babylon" as a coded name for Rome, from which Peter likely wrote. The city stood for the worldly power that oppressed the Christians.

pairs.left: The church elected together with you

pairs.right: Faithful brother by whom Peter wrote
